messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] MARS model predicting null
messages.body_plain:
Dear All

My pleasure to be in this elite group of R users.

This time my query relates to multivariate regression spline (MARS). While
using MARS for a set of 37, 63 and 100 number of samples (in rows) with 94,
147 and 265 species (in columns) respectively, and with a list of 24
predictors, I get NULL value with RSQ zero for these models.

Kindly help me understand why this happens even if the predictors selected
are ecologically significant and selected after removing collinearity (by
'usdm').

Best Regards
Rajendra M Panda
School of Water Resources
Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course: Introduction to GAM and GAMM
messages.subject_clean: Course: Introduction to GAM and GAMM
messages.body_plain:
We would like to announce the following statistics course:

Course: Introduction to GAM and GAMM
Where:  University of Konstanz, Konstanz, Germany
When:   7-11 March 2016

Course website: http://www.highstat.com/statscourse.htm
Course flyer: http://highstat.com/Courses/Flyer2016_03Konstanz.pdf



Kind regards,

Alain Zuur

-- 
Dr. Alain F. Zuur

First author of:
1. Beginner's Guide to GAMM with R (2014).
2. Beginner's Guide to GLM and GLMM with R (2013).
3. Beginner's Guide to GAM with R (2012).
4. Zero Inflated Models and GLMM with R (2012).
5. A Beginner's Guide to R (2009).
6. Mixed effects models and extensions in ecology with R (2009).
7. Analysing Ecological Data (2007).
